title: Civil-Military Relations
description: RAND research on civil-military relations includes studies of how a military employs civilian contractors, how military bases interact with their neighbors, and how a nation's military affects its politics—and vice versa.
text:
The UK's New Veterans Strategy Wants to Reshape Veterans' Roles in Society—Can It Succeed?
The United Kingdom's new Veterans Strategy aims to redefine how society sees those who served—shifting veterans from a vulnerable group to valued contributors to national security, resilience, and prosperity. Success will depend on changing perceptions, bridging policy gaps, and sustaining real commitment.
Nederland voorbereid: Lessen uit oorlogseconomieën voor een weerbare samenleving: [Netherlands prepared: Lessons from war economies for a resilient society]
RAND Europe's research maps out the consequences of a potential 'war economy' for the Ministry of Defence and society in general. This research focuses on the definition, stages, implications, challenges and international lessons.
Understanding the Transition from Military to Civilian Life
The Forces in Mind Trust (FiMT) commissioned a study by QinetiQ and RAND Europe to understand and improve the complex transition process from military to civilian life for UK Armed Forces personnel and their families.
Looking ahead: Enablers of innovation and scale for the future of Ukraine's defence-industrial base
Ukraine's defence industry is driven by skilled, combat-tested talent with strong state alignment. UAS tech leads globally, but high defence spend is unsustainable; future success hinges on exports powered by innovative capacity and skills.
Enabling progress in Armed Forces Covenant delivery at the local, regional and national level
This research brief presents the findings from the ‘Our Community, Our Covenant and beyond’ study, which examines the progress achieved and local realities of Armed Forces Covenant delivery across England, Scotland, and Wales.
Local approaches to Armed Forces Covenant delivery and wider support for the Armed Forces Community
This research brief examines local approaches to Armed Forces Covenant delivery areas across England, Scotland, and Wales. It draws on case study research conducted as part of the ‘Our Community, Our Covenant and beyond’ study.
Our Community, Our Covenant and beyond: Examining local realities and progress achieved in Armed Forces Covenant delivery
This study examines local delivery of the Armed Forces Covenant, including progress achieved to date as well as different local and regional delivery models, structures, and partnerships.
Local approaches to delivery of the Armed Forces Covenant and wider support for the Armed Forces Community: Findings from the Our Community, Our Covenant and beyond case studies
This case study research examines the implementation of the Armed Forces Covenant in five local authority areas across England, Scotland, and Wales, including the different delivery models used as well as identified cases of good practice.
Suppose They Held a War and Nobody Came: Systemic Approaches to Shared Military Personnel Challenges
Allies and partners of the United States are experiencing military personnel shortages caused by demographic decline, social factors, and economic conditions. In response, RAND researchers discuss eight options for systemic reform.
Our Community, Our Covenant and beyond
Forces in Mind Trust commissioned RAND Europe to research the local realities and progress made in the delivery of the Armed Forces Covenant.
Sustaining U.S. Army Operations in the Indo-Pacific: Potential Roles of Allies and Partners
Research shows that logistics and sustainment shortfalls could hamper U.S. Army operational success in the Indo-Pacific. In this report, the authors assess how allies and partners could support key military logistics and sustainment activities.
